race.name: Eislek Gravel
race.tagline: Luxembourg's UCI World Series race — a castle ramp, three forest laps, real climbing.

vitals.date_specific: 2027: June 20 (SUNDAY pattern; 2026 ran Sun Jun 21 — Wiebes/Vandeputte won in 37C heat)

climate.description: Mid-June in the Éislek: typically green and mild, but the 2026 edition was raced in 37°C heat, so real warm-weather days are on the table. Forest sections on the climbs provide shade; open stretches and the Our valley do not.
challenges: Potential for real heat (2026 edition raced at 37°C)
Forest shade on the climbs vs. exposed valley sections

terrain.surface: Laps of a 36.6-kilometre circuit out of Vianden in Luxembourg's Éislek/Ardennes — gravel and forest roads with a cobbled opening ramp. Every lap opens with a cobbled climb straight up to Vianden Castle, then roughly ten kilometres of climbing through forest to the lap's 510m high point before descending to the Our valley.
features: 109.8km / 2,670m for most categories (3 laps of 36.6km/~890m each)
Cobbled ramp to Vianden Castle opens every lap
~10km forest climb to the 510m high point per lap
Our valley descent closes every lap
UCI Gravel World Series round, Luxembourg-only since 2026

gravel_god_rating.score_note: Regional Luxembourg event. Rolling Ardennes terrain with moderate access, moderate costs, and regional field.

biased_opinion.summary: Eislek Gravel is a regional event in Luxembourg—rolling Ardennes terrain with moderate elevation. The Luxembourg Ardennes are scenic and organization is solid, but the moderate access and regional field keep it firmly in Tier 3.
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A scenic Luxembourg Ardennes gravel race. Worth it for European riders. Not practical for most international riders.

final_verdict.should_you_race: If you're in Europe—yes, it's solid. If you're international—there are better options.
final_verdict.alternatives: For better value: regional gravel races. For similar Ardennes terrain: Ardenne Gravel Stages.

logistics.food: Luxembourg has full dining options. Stock nutrition.

history.origin_story: A UCI Gravel World Series round organized by LetzXplore (Esch-sur-Sûre), built around the medieval town of Vianden in Luxembourg's Éislek/Ardennes. The course's signature is the cobbled ramp straight up to Vianden Castle, ridden at the start of every lap before roughly ten kilometres of forest climbing to the circuit's 510m high point. The inaugural 2025 edition ran cross-border with Germany; since 2026 the course runs entirely within Luxembourg.
notable_moments: 2025: inaugural edition sells out at 1,500 riders
2026 (2nd edition): Marianne Vos-era sprinters Lorena Wiebes and Niels Vandeputte win in 37°C heat
2026: course becomes Luxembourg-only (2025 was cross-border with Germany)
history.reputation: A UCI Gravel World Series qualifier known for its repeatable-climb format — the cobbled castle ramp and forest climb return every lap, so the race rewards riders who pace the first ascent knowing it's coming back twice more. A non-qualifying OPEN category shares the day with the UCI-qualifying field.

course_description.character: LetzXplore's UCI GWS round from Vianden: 36.6km loop — full course 3 laps = 109.8km/2,670m (68mi/8,760ft official; one UCI recap says ~2,350m — organizer figure primary) for most cats; 2 laps = 73.2km/1,780m official for men 60+/women 50+; plus non-qualifying OPEN category. Each lap: cobbled ramp to Vianden Castle, ~10km climb to the 510m high point through forest, descent past the Our valley. 2026+ runs ENTIRELY in Luxembourg (2025 inaugural was cross-border w/ Germany — do not describe as cross-border). RACE DAY SUNDAY. PRIOR 70mi/5,500ft mismatched — corrected 2026-07-14.

suffering_zones.label: Vianden Castle Ramp — Lap 1
suffering_zones.desc: Every lap opens with this: a cobbled ramp straight up to Vianden Castle, then roughly ten kilometres of forest climbing to the 510m high point. Fresh legs still make it feel manageable.
suffering_zones.terrain_detail: Cobbled ramp into sustained forest climbing

suffering_zones.label: Vianden Castle Ramp — Lap 2
suffering_zones.desc: The cobbles and the forest climb return whether you paced lap one honestly or not. This is where the field starts to sort itself.

suffering_zones.label: Vianden Castle Ramp — Lap 3
suffering_zones.desc: The final ascent, on legs that have already answered it twice. Whatever lap one overspent, lap three collects.
suffering_zones.terrain_detail: Same climb, third and final visit

course_description.signature_challenge: The cobbled ramp to Vianden Castle, followed by a ~10km forest climb to the 510m high point — repeated at the start of every lap.

guide_variables.race_terrain: 36.6km Vianden lap circuit — cobbled castle ramp + ~10km forest climb per lap
guide_variables.race_weather: Mid-June Éislek: typically mild-green, real heat possible (2026 edition raced at 37°C)
race_challenges: Cobbled Vianden Castle ramp + ~10km forest climb repeats every lap
Lap-race pacing discipline — lap one sets up laps two and three
Our valley descent on tired legs, three times
Potential real heat (2026 edition raced at 37°C)

non_negotiables.why: The cobbled Vianden Castle ramp + ~10km forest climb opens every lap — the third ascent has to cost what the first one did.
Each lap opens on cobbles before transitioning to forest gravel — technique under fatigue matters as much as fitness.
Lap racing punishes early aggression on the castle ramp — treat lap one as a loan against laps two and three.

topics.pacing: Lap-race pacing for a repeated climb

marketplace_variables.race_hook: UCI Gravel World Series lap race — the cobbled Vianden Castle ramp opens every lap.
marketplace_variables.race_hook_detail: 109.8km, three laps of a 36.6km Éislek circuit, each opening with a cobbled ramp to Vianden Castle then ~10km of forest climbing.

logistics.explanation: Start congestion forces walking up the opening climb - eins4eins reported "Bisschen nervig war auch der Stau an der steilen Rampe direkt nach dem Start. Durfte dann hoch laufen." Equipment failures compound problems: Loretta69 hit a branch that "hat mir hinten zwei Speichen weggehauen" with one spoke repeatedly catching in the disc brake.

length.explanation: The 109.8-kilometre (68-mile) distance demands careful pacing on the opening climb, and the opening climb repeats three times. Elite winners have raced it at speed averaging over 30 km/h across the full distance. Similar climbing density to Crusher in the Tushar, in a fraction of the distance.

technicality.explanation: Course opens with cobblestone climb to Vianden Castle, then hits a technical "grobes Geröll" (coarse scree) descent marked "Descent dangereuse!" Loretta69 notes the 400-500m section has "eine noch recht gut fahrbare Spur" on the right side. Surface composition runs 40% forest roads, 40% gravel roads, and 20% paved roads. Less technical than The Mid South.

elevation.explanation: Every lap climbs from Vianden (~200m) to the circuit's 510m high point via a cobbled castle ramp then roughly ten kilometres of forest gravel, with sections exceeding 10% gradient. The 109.8km course packs about 2,670 metres of climbing across all three laps. Riders have reported wishing for lower gearing than they brought. Comparable climbing density to The Mid South, in under half the distance.

climate.explanation: Historical mid-June averages run mild, but the 2026 edition was raced in 37°C heat, so real warm-weather days are on the table. Forest sections on the climbs provide tree cover relief, while the Our valley descents and open stretches do not. Similar heat-variability challenge to SBT GRVL.

altitude.explanation: Course peaks at just 510 meters in Luxembourg's northern forests. Rosa Klöser made a last-minute decision to enter less than a week after finishing the Tour de Suisse, showing zero acclimatization concerns for the modest elevation. Lower altitude than Gravel Worlds — no thin-air concerns.

adventure.explanation: The course loops out of medieval Vianden with a cobbled castle-ramp opener, forest climbing to the 510m high point, and a fast descent along the Our valley — three times. The Our valley sections run along elevated paths with real views. Since 2026 the course runs entirely within Luxembourg (the 2025 inaugural edition was cross-border with Germany).

prestige.explanation: UCI Gravel World Series qualifier attracted elite talent including Olympic champion Greg Van Avermaet, Petr Vakoc, and Niels Vandeputte. Winner Mathijs Loman noted the significance: "I'm excited to be part of history as the first winner of the Eislek Gravel" with 1,500 riders selling out the inaugural edition.

race_quality.explanation: Multi-lap circuit structure allows spectators multiple viewing opportunities through historic Vianden. Mathijs Loman praised the intensity: "It was an intense race right from the start" with the three-way sprint finish after 3:27:40 of racing. The course design creates natural selection through varied terrain.

experience.explanation: Cobblestone climb to medieval Vianden Castle creates a spectacular opening, while elevated paths overlook the Our River valley. Rosa Klöser emphasized the atmosphere: "The support from the crowd and my fellow competitors pushed me to give my best effort" in the historic Luxembourg setting.

community.explanation: eins4eins summarized the community feel: "War eine super Veranstaltung. Strecke war schwer aber hat Spaß gemacht, Vianden ist toll, alle freundlich vor Ort" (Great event. Course was hard but fun, Vianden is great, everyone friendly on site). Mathijs Loman noted "the atmosphere here in Luxembourg is electric."

field_depth.explanation: Elite fields included former Olympic champion Greg Van Avermaet, Niki Terpstra, and Annemarie Worst among notable talent. The lead group dynamics showed competitive depth, though Luxembourg's small population and inaugural status limited the overall participation pool to 1,500 riders. Less competitive depth than Almanzo 100. As Mathijs Loman puts it: "I can't believe it! It was an intense race right from the start, and I'm thrilled to have come out on top."

value.explanation: UCI World Series qualifier status with historic castle backdrop delivers solid racing value. eins4eins called it "eine super Veranstaltung" with challenging but enjoyable terrain. The 2026 expansion to two-day format with Gravel Village adds significant programming beyond the race itself.

expenses.explanation: Registration runs €50-80. Tire choice becomes expensive after course reconnaissance - Loretta69 switched from 40mm Hutchinson Caracal Race to Race King 2.2 MTB tires after pre-riding. Equipment demands include wider rubber and lower gearing than anticipated, plus Vianden's tourist destination status drives premium accommodation pricing. Higher total cost than Badlands.

citations.label: cyclingnews.com: Uci Gravel World Series Events 2025

primary.name: Specialized Pathfinder Pro
primary.why: Fast center, grippy edges. The modern all-rounder benchmark.

front_rear_split.rationale: Faster front for reduced rolling resistance on straights, grippier rear for traction on loose climbs and corners

videos.title: POV Inside Race Eislek Gravel Vianden 2025Eislek @visiteislek @EislekGravelLuxembourg
videos.description:
Begleite mich auf eine atemberaubende Fahrt beim Eislek Gravel Race in Luxemburg!  Erlebe die Schönheit des Eislek hautnah, während ich mich durch sommerliche Landschaften kämpfe und die Herausforderungen dieses epischen Rennens annehme.

In diesem POV Inside Race Video nehme ich dich mit auf:
Knackige Anstiege, die die Beine brennen lassen, aber mit unvergesslichen Ausblicken belohnen.
Historische Burgen, die als majestätische Kulisse dienen und dem Rennen eine besondere Note verleihen.
Rasante Abfahrten, bei denen Adrenalin pur durch die Adern schießt und volle Konzentration gefragt ist.
Temporeiche Streckenabschnitte, auf denen die Geschwindigkeit im Vordergrund steht.
